title: Rosa communis var. leiostyla
what_description: Rosa communis var. leiostyla is a botanical variety of the wild rose species, belonging to the family Rosaceae. It is a perennial shrub with a height of up to 2 meters, characterized by pinnate leaves with 7-11 leaflets, each 2-3 cm long and 1-2 cm wide, with serrated margins and a pointed tip. The leaves are dark green, with a petiole up to 2 cm long. The flowers are solitary, 4-6 cm in diameter, with 5-7 petals, and a yellow center surrounded by pink or reddish-purple sepals. The flowers are followed by red or purple hips, 1-2 cm in diameter, containing 10-20 seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Rosa communis var. leiostyla are inflammation, wounds, and fever, which are reduced by its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Fungal infections are treated with its antifungal compounds, while digestive issues and gastroenteritis are alleviated by its laxative and antiseptic properties. Its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties also help in healing skin irritations, cuts, and skin infections, while its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties promote wound healing and reduce pain. Additionally, its antiseptic properties aid in treating respiratory issues, cough, and skin ulcers, and its anti-inflammatory properties help in reducing skin irritations and eczema. Its antiseptic properties also treat insect bites, burns, and diarrhea, and its anti-anxiety properties help in reducing anxiety.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Rosa communis var. leiostyla are antioxidant, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, that neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Its antimicrobial properties are due to the presence of volatile oils, such as geraniol and linalool, that inhibit the growth of microorganisms and prevent infections. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory effects of this plant are attributed to the presence of saponins, which modulate the immune response and reduce inflammation. The analgesic properties of Rosa communis var. leiostyla are due to the presence of phenolic compounds, such as salicylic acid, that inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals. The cardioprotective properties of this plant are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which improve cardiovascular health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Rosa communis var. leiostyla are rosmarinic acid, quercetin, gallic acid, kaempferol, and ellagic acid. These flavonoids and phenolic acids have been identified as major antioxidants in the plant, exhibiting potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its healing properties. Rosmarinic acid has been shown to inhibit histamine release, reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, while quercetin has been found to have antiviral and anti-cancer activities. Gallic acid has been demonstrated to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, with kaempferol exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Ellagic acid has been found to have anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and to inhibit the growth of cancer cells.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Rosa communis var. leiostyla are rosehip tea, used to treat scurvy and other vitamin C deficiency disorders due to its high content of ascorbic acid, rosehip oil which is used topically for skin issues such as eczema and wounds due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, rosehip tincture used for similar purposes as the oil but in a more concentrated form, rosewater used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent for skin and eye irritations, and rose petal jam used for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties in the treatment of sore throats and mouth sores.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Rosa communis var. leiostyla improperly include inducing nausea due to its volatile oils, such as limonene and beta-pinene, which can cause gastrointestinal upset. Additionally, skin irritation may develop upon contact with the plant's sap, particularly in individuals with pre-existing skin conditions, due to the presence of phenolic compounds. Furthermore, improper use of Rosa communis var. leiostyla can induce allergic reactions, such as contact dermatitis and respiratory issues, in sensitized individuals. This is because the plant contains allergenic compounds like flavonoids and terpenes. Experience of nausea and allergic reactions can also be attributed to the improper identification and handling of the plant, which may lead to accidental ingestion or exposure to its toxic constituents.
